questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The slope of a line is given by the formula m = (y2 - y1)/(x2 - x1).
questions.reason: Reason (R): This formula is derived from the concept of the gradient of a line.

(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A

(D) A is false but R is true

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The slope of a line is a measure of its steepness.

(C) A is true but R is false

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The equation of a line in the slope-intercept form is y = mx + c.

(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
